question: Your ________ is your ability to see.
If you are ________, you can't tell the difference between some colours.
________ are the tiny bumps on your tongue that allow you to recognise flavours.
Which word means 'a pleasant smell of flowers'?
The word 'aroma' describes:
choices: the smell of something delicious cooking

question: Which adjective means 'covered in small, short pointed spines or points'?
The word 'subtle' describes something that is:
Which word means 'soft and smooth like the material silk'?
In an emergency, you can't stop ________ questions.
The animal would need ________ an X-ray for confirmation.
I remember ________ my swimming kit, but now I can't find it.
Remember ________ your swimming kit before you leave.
I'll never forget ________ ice cream for the first time.
Don't forget ________ the ice cream in the freezer.
Becoming a perfumer means ________ up spicy food.
I didn't mean ________ so much chilli sauce on my chips.
My contact lenses felt weird at first, but I ________ wearing them eventually.
He ________ sleeping on a hard mattress — it doesn't bother him.
I ________ eating fish, but I love it now.
Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'used to' for a past habit that no longer exists.
choices: I am used to wake up early.
I used to wake up early.
I get used to waking up early.
I used to waking up early.

question: I saw him ________ to his friend while they were waiting outside.
I saw somebody ________ in the water.
The kitchen really needs ________. It hasn't been cleaned in weeks.
These documents require ________ before they can be processed.

Read the passage: 'Sabriye Tenberken became blind at the age of 12. She went to a special boarding school for the blind where she learnt horse riding and how to read Braille.' How old was Sabriye when she became blind?

Read the passage: 'Sabriye went to a special boarding school for the blind. She learnt various sports including horse riding and she also learnt how to read Braille.' What did she learn at the special boarding school?

Read the passage: 'There was no Braille in Tibetan, so she decided to write a Tibetan Braille system. At first, she did this to help her with her own studies, but then she realised its potential to help people in the mountainous region of China.' Why did Sabriye originally write a Tibetan Braille system?
choices: to help with her own studies

question: Read the passage: 'Many Tibetan people had problems with their eyesight, partly because of their diet but mainly because of the damaging sun at high altitudes.' What is the MAIN reason for eyesight problems in Tibet?
choices: the damaging sun at high altitudes

question: Read the passage: 'Her work has become famous and she was even nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize in 2005.' What was Sabriye nominated for?

Read the passage: 'Together they set up a school, called Braille Without Borders. It has a vision to help blind children to adjust to their blindness and to learn many of the same skills that she had learnt as a girl.' What is the name of the school Sabriye and Paul set up?
Which word describes food that is 'delicious-tasting and makes your mouth water'?
Which of the following is NOT a sound word?
I'd rather ________ something light this evening.
I prefer ________ at home to going out to a restaurant.
Which phrase gives someone else the choice?
choices: Given the choice, I'd go there.

question: In a review, where do you give brief details of where and when you went and your overall impression?
Which intensifier can ONLY be used with non-gradable adjectives?
Which intensifier can be used with BOTH gradable and non-gradable adjectives?
